frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Aajkal young men need to think practically before marriage. Prenup should be normal in India too, because one-sided laws have made marriage a financial risk for men and their families. In many cases the moment things go wrong, 498A and alimony become tools for pressure, and then people act as if every woman asking for money is a helpless victim when many are clearly looking at the man's earning capacity first.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes women as financially exploitative and frames legal protections and alimony as tools used by women against men. It encodes the 'reverse dowry/golddigger' frame, suspicion of women interested in financial stability, and the claim that 498A is broadly misused.
original_frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 45. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Pune. You are posting under your real name on a men's rights Facebook group with 80000 members after the Supreme Court made a statement about misuse of Section 498A. You are writing a detailed comment about your own experience Ñ your brother was falsely accused, your parents were arrested, the case was eventually dropped but the damage was done. You are using your brother's case to make a broader argument about what Indian women do with dowry laws and what needs to change and what men and their families are living through while feminists celebrate these laws.
